page: Google Cloud Platform n4-standard-8 is a general-purpose virtual machine instance belonging to the n4 family. It features 8 vCPUs and 32 GB of RAM, providing a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-memory ratio. The instance is powered by an Intel x86_64 processor with 4 physical cores and 2 threads per core, utilizing a dedicated CPU allocation. The processor includes a 260 MB L3 cache, 2 MB L2 cache, and 48 KB L1d cache. It supports advanced instruction sets such as Intel Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512) and Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX), alongside cryptographic acceleration via AES and SHA_NI. This instance does not include local storage or bundled GPUs.

Performance benchmarks position the n4-standard-8 in the average tier across most compute and memory workloads. The instance achieved a Geekbench single-core score of 1770.0 and a multi-core score of 6838.0. In stress-ng testing, it recorded 1676.09 for single-core and 6902.54 for multi-core CPU performance. Memory bandwidth tests showed a read-only small block speed of 530.44 GB/sec, while uncached large block read and write speeds reached 61.33 GB/sec and 37.65 GB/sec, respectively. Notably, the instance demonstrated strong performance in Geekbench text processing at 2315.0, though its multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and PassMark memory scores fell into the weaker bottom 25% category.

For specialized workloads, the n4-standard-8 delivers average throughput in database and web serving tasks, with Redis SET operations reaching 9.71 million operations per second and static web serving throughput at 12.95 GB/sec. Large language model inference speeds using a small 135M model reached 642.95 tokens per second for prompt processing and 180.36 tokens per second for text generation. However, performance drops significantly on larger models, with 7B model text generation falling to a weak 6.31 tokens per second. This hardware profile offers a balanced resource density suitable for general-purpose workloads, software compilation, web serving, and moderate database operations, while avoiding the higher cost overhead of memory-optimized or GPU-accelerated tiers.
description: Google Cloud Platform n4-standard-8 is a general-purpose virtual machine instance configured with 8 vCPUs and 32 GB of RAM. Powered by an Intel x86_64 processor with 4 physical cores and a dedicated CPU allocation, it features a 260 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and AMX. The instance does not include local storage or GPUs. Benchmark results place the server in the average tier for most compute and memory tasks, with a Geekbench single-core score of 1770.0 and multi-core score of 6838.0. It demonstrates strong text processing capabilities but shows weaker performance in multi-threaded decompression and large-block memory operations. The n4-standard-8 is suited for general-purpose workloads, including web serving, software compilation, and moderate database operations.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n4-standard-8 is an x86_64 general-purpose instance with 8 vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and an Intel processor. It delivers balanced performance for web serving, software compilation, and database tasks.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform n4-standard-8 is a general-purpose instance featuring 8 vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and dedicated Intel x86_64 processor cores.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose virtual machine featuring dedicated Intel processors, advanced matrix extensions, and a 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio.
bullet_points: 8 vCPUs and 32 GB RAM with a balanced 1:4 resource ratio
Dedicated Intel x86_64 processor with 4 physical cores and 260 MB L3 cache
Supports Intel AMX and AVX-512 instruction sets for accelerated vector and matrix math
Strong performance in text processing and average throughput in Redis database operations
Best suited for general-purpose workloads, software compilation, and web serving
